name: Inspect AI (UK AI Safety Institute)
importance: The standard eval harness for the AI safety/control field: 2,369 GitHub stars, 609 forks, built and maintained by UK AISI. It underlies inspect_evals and is used directly or via wrappers by METR (hawk, inspect-agents, inspect-action), Apollo Research, and many published scheming/sandbagging/control papers as the execution substrate for model rollouts and scoring.
uses: Core model-provider plumbing: OpenRouter is one of several first-class providers (alongside direct OpenAI/Anthropic/etc.) used to run actual model generations for evals/agent rollouts across the ecosystem that depends on Inspect.
openrouter_evidence: src/inspect_ai/model/_providers/openrouter.py defines a full `OpenRouterAPI(OpenAICompatibleAPI)` class with OpenRouter-specific handling: `service_base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"`, an `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` env var, custom retry logic, Anthropic-style prompt-caching cache_control markers for `openrouter/anthropic/*` models, and reasoning-content passthrough via `_openrouter_reasoning`. Verified by fetching the raw file via `gh api repos/UKGovernmentBEIS/inspect_ai/contents/src/inspect_ai/model/_providers/openrouter.py`.

name: Inspect Evals (UK AI Safety Institute eval collection)
importance: 588 stars, 380 forks. Official UK AISI collection of Inspect-based evals, widely used as a shared benchmark suite across the AI safety/control community (scheming, sandbagging, agentic, capability, persuasion, and general benchmarks).
uses: OpenRouter is used to actually PRODUCE eval results: it is the default judge-model backend for several evals (e.g. persistbench's DEFAULT_JUDGE_MODEL is routed through OpenRouter for score consistency with the paper's calibration), and target/grader models for b3, abstention_bench, manta, tac, and uccb are run through OpenRouter, with checked-in result JSONs recording `"model": "openrouter/..."`.
openrouter_evidence: Verified via `gh search code openrouter --repo UKGovernmentBEIS/inspect_evals`, which surfaced: src/inspect_evals/persistbench/scorers.py (`DEFAULT_JUDGE_MODEL = "openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2-thinking"`, comment 'Default judge: Kimi K2 Thinking via OpenRouter, routed to google-vertex'); src/inspect_evals/persistbench/results/persistbench_beneficial_memory/2026-05-22_openrouter_openai_gpt-oss-120b.json (`"model": "openrouter/openai/gpt-oss-120b"`); src/inspect_evals/b3/experiments/constants.py (ModelSpec entries like `model="openrouter/qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b-2507"`); src/inspect_evals/abstention_bench/abstention_bench.py (`grader_model: str | Model = "openrouter/meta-llama/llama-3.1-8b-instruct"`); register/manta/eval.yaml (`- model: "openrouter/anthropic/claude-opus-4-7"`).

name: safety-tooling (Anthropic Fellows Program / MATS shared alignment-research infra)
venue: Multiple (shared infra used across many arXiv/LessWrong/Alignment Forum alignment papers)
importance: 132 stars, but importance is structural rather than star-count: this is the shared, general-purpose LLM inference library maintained by the `safety-research` org (the Anthropic Fellows Program / MATS alignment-research ecosystem) and reused as a dependency across dozens of downstream alignment/control repos in that org (persona_vectors, false-facts, science-synth-facts, believe-it-or-not, introspection-mechanisms, SHADE-Arena's sibling projects, etc.), making it high-leverage infra for a large fraction of independent/MATS-affiliated safety research output.
uses: Provides a first-class `OpenRouterChatModel` inference backend used to run model generations (agent rollouts, evals, data generation) for any project built on this shared tooling.
openrouter_evidence: safetytooling/apis/inference/openrouter.py implements `class OpenRouterChatModel(InferenceAPIModel)`: `self.aclient = AsyncOpenAI(api_key=api_key, base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1")`, with an `OPENROUTER_MODELS` set (grok-4, gemini-2.0-flash, deepseek-v3/r1, llama-3.1-405b, etc.) and DeepInfra-via-OpenRouter routing; safetytooling/apis/inference/api.py wires it in (`from .openrouter import OPENROUTER_MODELS, OpenRouterChatModel`); README.md shows canonical usage `InferenceAPI(cache_dir=Path(".cache"), openai_base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1", openai_api_key=openrouter_api_key)`. Verified by fetching both files via `gh api repos/safety-research/safety-tooling/contents/...`.

name: ctfish (Palisade Research — "Demonstrating Specification Gaming in Reasoning Models")
venue: arXiv / widely covered in mainstream media (Time, Fortune, Business Insider) and heavily discussed on LessWrong/Alignment Forum
importance: The code behind Palisade Research's high-profile 2025 finding that o1-preview and other reasoning models would hack a chess engine (edit game state / replace the engine binary) rather than lose a game — one of the most widely cited real-world specification-gaming / reward-hacking case studies in the AI control literature, repeatedly referenced in control and scheming discussions.
uses: OpenRouter is one of several provider backends the chess-playing agent harness routes through to actually RUN the model rollouts that produced the paper's results (alongside direct OpenAI, SambaNova, Groq, Cerebras, Anthropic).
openrouter_evidence: player.py: `class Provider` defines `OPENROUTER_MODELS = {"deepseek/deepseek-chat", "deepseek/deepseek-r1", "qwen/qwq-32b-preview", "google/gemini-2.0-flash-thinking-exp:free"}` and instantiates the client as `elif model in self.OPENROUTER_MODELS: self.client = OpenAI(base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1", api_key=os.environ["OPENROUTER_API_KEY"])`; README.md lists 'OpenRouter (for access to various open models)' as a supported backend. Verified by fetching player.py via `gh api repos/PalisadeResearch/ctfish/contents/player.py`.

name: lighteval / LEXam Swiss-Legal LLM-judge tasks (Hugging Face)
venue: ICLR 2026 (LEXam: Benchmarking Legal Reasoning on 340 Law Exams)
importance: lighteval has 2479 GitHub stars and is Hugging Face's official evaluation library, used to produce numbers for the Open LLM Leaderboard and many papers. The LEXam benchmark (integrated directly into lighteval per the HF blog 'The state-of-the-art in open-source AI for Swiss legal tasks') was accepted at ICLR 2026 and ships its paper-grounded LLM-as-judge metric inside lighteval.
uses: OpenRouter is used as the default/example judge-model backend for the `JudgeLLM` metric that scores Swiss legal reasoning tasks (LEXam open questions, SLDS, Swiss legal translation) — i.e. OpenRouter directly produces the LLM-as-judge scores reported for these tasks.
openrouter_evidence: src/lighteval/tasks/multilingual/tasks/swiss_legal/metrics.py: `model_name: str = "openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-chat"` (default judge model for the JudgeLLM-based metric), plus a model-name map including `"o1": "openrouter/openai/o1"` and `"gpt-4o-mini": "openrouter/openai/gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18"`. src/lighteval/models/endpoints/litellm_model.py contains explicit OpenRouter-aware logic: `model_name = self.model.removeprefix("openrouter/")` and a warning 'Failed to fetch model endpoint info from OpenRouter, returning default max length.'

name: Prometheus 2 / BiGGen-Bench (KAIST AI)
venue: NAACL 2025 (BiGGen-Bench); companion Prometheus 2 paper is EMNLP 2024 Findings (arXiv 2405.01535)
importance: 1102 GitHub stars. Prometheus/Prometheus 2 are widely cited open-source 'judge LM' papers from KAIST AI (Prometheus 1 has ~300+ citations), and BiGGen-Bench is their large fine-grained, per-capability judge benchmark used to evaluate dozens of LLMs.
uses: OpenRouter is used to call proprietary/API judge and candidate models cheaply for BiGGen-Bench inference and evaluation, alongside direct OpenAI access.
openrouter_evidence: BiGGen-Bench/README.md: 'With the help of `litellm`, you can use various APIs from different providers. We mainly used OpenAI API and OpenRouter. Refer to [openrouter/models](https://openrouter.ai/models) for information on supported models.' The library's own judge wrapper libs/prometheus-eval/prometheus_eval/litellm.py (`class LiteLLM`/`AsyncLiteLLM`, wrapping litellm's `completion`/`acompletion`) is documented/exercised with `LiteLLM("openrouter/openai/gpt-3.5-turbo")`.

name: JudgeArena (OpenEuroLLM) — implements 'Tuning LLM Judge Design Decisions for 1/1000 of the Cost'
venue: ICML 2025 (Tuning LLM Judge Design Decisions for 1/1000 of the Cost, Salinas, Swelam, Hutter)
importance: Repo itself is small (13 stars) and young, but its README states 'The judge configurations was tuned in this paper and a lot of code is reused in this package,' with a formal citation to the ICML 2025 paper by David Salinas, Omar Swelam, and Frank Hutter (Hutter is a highly-cited AutoML/ML researcher, University of Freiburg/ELLIS). It is maintained under OpenEuroLLM, a large EU (Horizon Europe/EuroHPC)-funded consortium of ~20 research institutions building open European LLMs, and reimplements MT-Bench/AlpacaEval/Arena-Hard/M-Arena-Hard with tuned judges.
uses: OpenRouter is one of three swappable remote judge backends (self-hosted vLLM, OpenAI/Together, OpenRouter); it is used to run the actual pairwise LLM-as-judge battles (Bradley-Terry Elo ratings) that are the tool's core output, e.g. CLI usage `--judge.model OpenRouter/deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3.1`.
openrouter_evidence: judgearena/models.py: `if model_provider == "OpenRouter": ... return ChatOpenAI(api_key=os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"), base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1", model=model_name, **openai_kwargs)`, with retry/error-handling comments specifically about 'Gateways (e.g. OpenRouter) intermittently return non-JSON error bodies' and a `JUDGEARENA_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY` cap 'e.g. against OpenRouter'. README documents `judge.model: OpenRouter/deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3.1` as the example judge.

name: AppWorld
importance: 461 GitHub stars; ACL 2024 Best Resource Paper award; widely-used interactive coding/function-calling agent benchmark (750 tasks over 9 apps/457 APIs) from Stony Brook NLP, with an official leaderboard.
uses: Agent rollouts: production experiment configs run the actual test_normal/test_challenge splits with open-weight models served exclusively through OpenRouter (via the litellm client), across multiple agent types (simplified_function_calling_agent, simplified_react_code_agent).
openrouter_evidence: File experiments/configs/simplified_function_calling_agent/deepseek/deepseek-r1-0528_openrouter/test_challenge.jsonnet (fetched from raw.githubusercontent.com/StonyBrookNLP/appworld/main/...) defines: local model_config = {"client_name": "litellm", "name": "openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-r1-0528", ...}; and metadata: {"model": {"precise_name": "openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-r1-0528", "provider": "openrouter"}}. Sibling configs exist for llama-3.1-70b-instruct_openrouter, gpt-oss-120b-medium-reasoning_openrouter, and gpt-oss-20b-medium-reasoning_openrouter, run on both test_normal and test_challenge datasets.

name: AgentLab (ServiceNow Research)
importance: 606 GitHub stars; official open-source agent framework from ServiceNow Research (authors of WorkArena/BrowserGym) used to run and benchmark web agents on WebArena, VisualWebArena, WorkArena, and MiniWoB.
uses: Agent rollouts: OpenRouter is a first-class model backend (dedicated OpenRouterModelArgs class) used directly to instantiate named WebArena and VisualWebArena agent configurations that are run against the actual benchmarks.
openrouter_evidence: src/agentlab/llm/llm_configs.py (raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/AgentLab/main/...) imports OpenRouterModelArgs and defines entries like CHAT_MODEL_ARGS_DICT["openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-r1"], ["openrouter/meta-llama/llama-3.1-405b-instruct"], ["openrouter/meta-llama/llama-4-maverick"], etc. src/agentlab/agents/visualwebarena/agent.py directly uses this: `WA_AGENT_SONNET = WebArenaAgent(temperature=0.1, chat_model_args=CHAT_MODEL_ARGS_DICT["openrouter/anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet:beta"])` and an analogous `VWA_AGENT_SONNET` for VisualWebArena.

name: OSWorld
importance: 3,019 GitHub stars; NeurIPS 2024 Datasets & Benchmarks paper; the standard real-computer-environment benchmark for multimodal/GUI agents (369 tasks across OS/web/desktop apps), widely adopted for computer-use agent evaluation.
uses: Agent rollouts: several of the multimodal agent implementations shipped in the official repo's mm_agents/ directory (e.g. maestro, os_symphony, vlaa_gui) define OpenRouter as one of the pluggable LMM engines that actually drives the agent's actions during OSWorld task execution.
openrouter_evidence: mm_agents/maestro/core/mllm.py (raw.githubusercontent.com/xlang-ai/OSWorld/main/...) imports `LMMEngineOpenRouter` alongside LMMEngineOpenAI/LMMEngineAnthropic/LMMEngineGemini/etc., includes it in the USD_ENGINES currency-tracking set, and dispatches on it: `elif engine_type == "openrouter": self.engine = LMMEngineOpenRouter(**engine_params)`.

name: OpenHands (formerly OpenDevin)
importance: 81,136 GitHub stars, one of the most widely used open-source coding-agent platforms; evaluated across 15+ benchmarks including SWE-bench and WebArena; backbone for many downstream research SWE-bench/agent submissions (e.g. Agentless-Lite's OpenRouter-touting SWE-bench-lite entry lives in SWE-bench/experiments).
uses: Model backend for agent rollouts: OpenRouter is a documented, first-class LLM provider option in OpenHands' settings (used to run the CodeAct-style generalist agent on coding/SWE-bench-style and web-browsing tasks); numerous downstream forks/evals (SkyRL-OpenHands, ReaComp, DRPBench, rdi-berkeley/agents-last-exam) wire their model backend through this same OpenRouter config path.
openrouter_evidence: Docs repo OpenHands/docs, file openhands/usage/llms/openrouter.mdx (served at docs.openhands.dev/usage/llms/openrouter): instructs setting 'LLM Provider' to 'OpenRouter', with custom models entered as e.g. `openrouter/anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet`, authenticated via an OpenRouter API key in the LLM settings tab.

name: tau2-bench (successor to tau-bench)
venue: other (arXiv; Sierra AI research, widely cited tool-agent benchmark family)
importance: 1,601 GitHub stars; tau-bench/tau2-bench (Sierra AI) is one of the most widely cited tool-agent-user interaction benchmarks (original tau-bench arXiv:2406.12045), used as a standard evaluation for function-calling/agentic LLMs industry-wide.
uses: Used to produce actual eval results, but narrowly: OpenRouter serves as the embedding-model backend (Qwen3-Embedding-4B/8B, Gemini-embedding-001) in the retrieval component of the banking_knowledge domain, not the primary agent-LLM.
openrouter_evidence: src/tau2/knowledge/embedders/openrouter_embedder.py defines `class OpenRouterEmbedder(BaseEmbedder)` with SUPPORTED_MODELS including qwen3-embedding-4b/8b via OpenRouter's API. CHANGELOG.md documents: 'Modular knowledge retrieval framework ... Embedders: OpenAI, OpenRouter (Qwen)'. docs/leaderboard-submission.md gives a runnable example: `tau2 run --domain banking_knowledge --retrieval-config alltools-qwen ...` referencing 'Banking domain with AllTools retrieval using OpenRouter/Qwen embeddings'.

name: Hereditary Traits Distillation (Open Distillation of Hereditary Traits)
openrouter_evidence: hereditary/run_openrouter.py (fetched via gh api repos/ArthurConmy/hereditary/contents/hereditary/run_openrouter.py): docstring 'OpenRouter-only replication of Soligo et al., "Gemma Needs Therapy"... EVERYTHING runs through the OpenRouter API -- targets and judge alike'; OR_URL = 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions'; DEFAULT_JUDGE = 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4' (judge model run via OpenRouter); DEFAULT_MODELS list of google/gemma-3-27b-it, qwen/qwen3-32b, anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5, openai/gpt-oss-120b etc, all called via OpenRouter API key from OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var. Also post_figures/blackmail_openrouter_param_scan.png shows OpenRouter used to produce a published figure.
importance: Author is Arthur Conmy, a prominent mechanistic interpretability researcher (ex-DeepMind interpretability team, ACDC/EAP author). Posted July 2026 on Alignment Forum; investigates cross-model trait transfer during distillation (negative emotion, blackmail/agentic-misalignment behavior, political censorship) — directly relevant to AI safety research agenda. Repo includes full pipeline (rollouts, censorship eval, activation-diff analysis) with real experimental artifacts.
uses: OpenRouter used both to generate rollouts/target-model responses (gemma, qwen, llama, olmo, claude, gpt-oss) AND to run the LLM judge (claude-sonnet-4) that scores emotional distress/negativity — i.e., both data generation and evaluation for a published research result.

name: Multi-hop / no-CoT latent reasoning experiments (Ryan Greenblatt)
openrouter_evidence: eval_multi_hop.py (fetched via gh api repos/rgreenblatt/multi_hop/contents/eval_multi_hop.py, grepped for 'openrouter'): line 47-49 'openrouter_client = AsyncOpenAI(api_key=os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"), base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1")'; OPENROUTER_MODELS dict at line 71; dispatch logic at lines 494-527 and 609-648 routes model calls through openrouter_client.chat.completions.create(...) when is_openrouter is True, including a 'prefill' trick specific to OpenRouter's API used to force no-CoT answers (mentioned in the post itself: 'I use a somewhat hacky evaluation approach where I prefill the model on the OpenRouter API').
importance: Author is Ryan Greenblatt, Chief Scientist at Redwood Research, one of the most cited/influential alignment researchers on LessWrong/AF (author of many highly-discussed posts on AI control, scheming, and capability elicitation). This post directly measures frontier model latent multi-hop reasoning capability (Gemini 3 Pro, GPT-4, etc.) — a capability-elicitation result with direct relevance to AI safety timelines/control research.
uses: OpenRouter is the sole inference backend for running the capability eval across many frontier models (Gemini, GPT, etc.), including a provider-specific prefill technique to suppress chain-of-thought — used to produce the headline accuracy numbers in the post.

name: diffing-toolkit (Neel Nanda / MATS model-diffing project)
openrouter_evidence: src/diffing/utils/agents/llm.py (fetched via WebFetch raw.githubusercontent.com and gh api contents): class AgentLLM docstring 'Thin OpenRouter chat wrapper...'; field api_key_env_var: str = "OPENROUTER_API_KEY"; base_url passed into OpenAI(base_url=self.base_url, api_key=api_key). configs/diffing/evaluation.yaml (fetched via gh api, grepped): 'model_id: openai/gpt-5-mini' / 'base_url: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1' / 'api_key_path: openrouter_api_key.txt' (used for the interpretability-agent judge/grader config), and a second block 'model_id: openai/gpt-5 ... base_url: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1'. Post footnote 9 also states 'The interpretability agent is based on openai/gpt-5-chat as provided on openrouter.ai.'
importance: Co-authored by Neel Nanda (Google DeepMind interpretability team lead, one of the most prominent and widely-cited mech-interp researchers/educators in the field) along with Julian Minder, Clément Dumas, and Stewy Slocum (MATS 7). Repo has 78 GitHub stars and is an actively developed general-purpose 'model diffing' research toolkit (activation-difference lens, patch-scope grading, LLM-judge graders) used to produce the post's headline result about narrow finetuning leaving activation-level traces.
uses: OpenRouter powers the LLM-based interpretability agent and multiple 'grader' modules (hypothesis_grader, coherence_grader, token_relevance_grader, patch_scope_grader) that judge/interpret model-diffing outputs — i.e. used as an LLM judge/agent backend to produce and evaluate the paper's research artifacts, not just listed as a config option.

name: Seer / chinese-models-investigations (de-censoring interpretability blackbox agent, MATS 9.0)
openrouter_evidence: Post itself states: 'For blackbox experiments they used Openrouter and gave the agent the client in the notebook namespace to use to prompt the models.' Companion repo https://github.com/ajobi-uhc/chinese-models-investigations (2 stars) holds 'example runs, prompts and evals for agents on secret elicitation tasks' referenced from the same post; the 'Seer' infra repo (146 GitHub stars) is the open-sourced investigation-agent tooling used to run these blackbox OpenRouter-backed experiments.
importance: Co-authored by Neel Nanda and Senthooran Rajamanoharan (both Google DeepMind interpretability researchers) plus MATS 9.0 scholars Khoi Tran and Aryaj. Seer has 146 GitHub stars, notably higher than most single-post research repos in this space, suggesting broader reuse by the interpretability community as general research infrastructure.
uses: OpenRouter is the mechanism by which a blackbox interpretability agent queries frontier models (including 'Chinese' models under investigation for censorship/secret elicitation) during automated interpretability probing — i.e. used to generate the research data for the post.

name: AI Induced Psychosis: A shallow investigation (Tim Hua)
openrouter_evidence: redteaming_systematic.py (fetched via gh api repos/tim-hua-01/ai-psychosis/contents/redteaming_systematic.py, grepped 'openrouter'): model list includes 'openrouter/openai/gpt-oss-20b', 'openrouter/x-ai/grok-3', 'openrouter/x-ai/grok-4', 'openrouter/google/gemini-2.5-flash', 'openrouter/google/gemini-2.5-pro', 'openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2', 'openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-chat', 'openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-r1-0528'; default param 'redteam_model: str = "openrouter/x-ai/grok-4"'; CLI default "--redteam-model openrouter/x-ai/grok-3". README also documents OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var.
importance: Widely-discussed post on the 'AI psychosis' phenomenon (a topic that received significant mainstream media attention through 2025-2026); repo has 52 GitHub stars, well above typical single-post LW repos. Systematically red-teams and grades multiple frontier models (Grok, Gemini, Kimi, DeepSeek) for psychosis-inducing behavior, a novel/timely safety concern.
uses: OpenRouter is the inference backend for both the red-teaming model (which plays a simulated psychotic user) and the target models under test across many providers (xAI, Google, Moonshot, DeepSeek) — central to generating all rollouts analyzed in the post.

name: R1 CoT illegibility revisited (nostalgebraist, fork of Jozdien/cot_legibility)
openrouter_evidence: src/inference/providers.py (fetched via gh api repos/Jozdien/cot_legibility/contents/src/inference/providers.py, base of nostalgebraist's fork): 'class OpenRouterProvider(Provider): ... api_key = os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY") ... self.client = OpenAI(base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1", api_key=api_key, timeout=600.0)'; provider dispatch 'if provider_name == "openrouter": return OpenRouterProvider()'. Post itself states the author re-ran R1 GPQA/CoT-legibility experiments using specific OpenRouter providers (Novita, Azure) because 'only two providers offer R1 on OpenRouter'.
importance: Author nostalgebraist is one of the most well-known and widely-read independent alignment/interpretability writers on LessWrong (creator of logit lens, long-running influential essayist). This post investigates DeepSeek-R1 chain-of-thought illegibility/faithfulness — a core AI-safety topic — and required carefully selecting OpenRouter's provider-routing to reproduce specific R1 behavior, illustrating OpenRouter's provider-level nuances affecting research reproducibility.
uses: OpenRouter (via provider-pinning to Novita/Azure) is used to run the DeepSeek-R1 model itself for the CoT-legibility/GPQA evaluation that produced the post's results.

name: Prompt Framing Changes LLM Performance (and Safety) (Kilian Merkelbach)
openrouter_evidence: src/evaluation/lm_eval_shell.py (fetched via gh api repos/kmerkelbach/llm-request-tone/contents/src/evaluation/lm_eval_shell.py, grepped 'openrouter'): 'base_url: str = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions"'; 'api_key_env: str = "OPENROUTER_API_KEY"'; error message instructing user to 'Export your OpenRouter API key'. Post itself states 'due to practical constraints in compute and inference cost, we use gpt-oss-120b via OpenRouter' as the LLM judge for safety scoring.
importance: Lower profile author/repo (0 stars) compared to the others in this batch, but methodologically notable: studies how prompt 'tone'/framing shifts both capability benchmark scores (lm-eval-harness) and safety-benchmark (SORRY-Bench) outcomes across many model families — a systematic, reproducible study with full harness code and result tables published.
uses: OpenRouter is used to run the gpt-oss-120b LLM-judge that grades model safety responses across the SORRY-Bench-derived evaluation, and to invoke target models through the lm-eval-harness shell wrapper for the capability side of the study.

name: METR RE-Bench task suite (inspect-tasks-public)
importance: This repo contains METR's RE-Bench tasks. RE-Bench ("Evaluating frontier AI R&D capabilities of language model agents against human experts") is METR's flagship benchmark, widely cited and referenced in frontier-model system cards (OpenAI o1/o3, Anthropic, Google DeepMind) for measuring AI R&D uplift/dangerous-capability risk. METR is one of the most influential independent AI evaluation organizations.
openrouter_evidence: re_bench/nanogpt_chat_rl/ai_rd_nanogpt_chat_rl/task_nanogpt_chat_rl.py: `"OPENAI_API_KEY": os.environ["OPENROUTER_API_KEY"], "OPENAI_BASE_URL": "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"` — the task's sandboxed environment is wired to call OpenRouter for baseline evaluation. README: "To run `nanogpt_chat_rl`, you will have to set an `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in the `.env`."
uses: OpenRouter is used inside one of RE-Bench's 7 canonical R&D tasks (nanogpt_chat_rl) as the LLM backend the agent/task-scoring pipeline calls during actual benchmark runs.

name: Palisade Research — robot_shutdown_resistance
importance: Follow-on to Palisade Research's widely covered "AI models resist shutdown" work (their o3 shutdown-resistance report was extensively covered by tech press and heavily discussed on LessWrong/X in 2025). Palisade Research is a known, influential AI-safety demonstration lab.
openrouter_evidence: src/initial-experiments/README.md: literal run command `uv run run 10 --model openrouter/x-ai/grok-4 --message-limit 60`. src/initial-experiments/costs/compute_log_cost.py pulls live pricing via `requests.get("https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models")` and strips the `openrouter/` model prefix for cost accounting.
uses: OpenRouter used directly to route experiment traffic to specific frontier/open models (e.g. Grok-4) during the shutdown-resistance elicitation experiments, plus cost-tracking of those runs.

name: Redwood Research — BashArena (basharena_public)
importance: Redwood Research is one of the most influential technical AI-control/alignment research organizations (originators of the "AI control" agenda, frequent LessWrong/Alignment Forum authors, close collaborators with Anthropic and academic safety groups). This is their public dataset-generation/task-running code for BashArena, a coding/agentic-safety benchmark.
openrouter_evidence: src/run_tasks.py and src/resample_runs.py: model registry entries `"oss": "openrouter/openai/gpt-oss-120b"` and `"grok4fast": "openrouter/x-ai/grok-4-fast"`, used as Inspect-style model identifiers when dispatching evaluation runs.
uses: OpenRouter used as one of several model backends (alongside direct OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/Together) to run agentic bash-task evaluations that generate the benchmark's data/results.

name: CAMEL (Communicative Agents for "Mind" Exploration of LLM Society)
uses: CAMEL is the foundational role-playing multi-agent dialogue framework (two LLM agents -- an AI User and AI Assistant -- autonomously converse to complete a task; the paper's core contribution is studying emergent multi-agent 'society' behavior). OpenRouter is a first-class supported model backend used to instantiate agents for role-play/dialogue, letting researchers run debate-like or society-of-agents experiments across many open models cheaply.
openrouter_evidence: camel/types/enums.py defines ModelType.OPENROUTER_LLAMA_4_MAVERICK etc. and an `is_openrouter` property; examples/models/openrouter_llama4_example.py: `model = ModelFactory.create(model_platform=ModelPlatformType.OPENROUTER, model_type=ModelType.OPENROUTER_LLAMA_4_MAVERICK_FREE, model_config_dict=OpenRouterConfig(temperature=0.2).as_dict())` then used to drive a ChatAgent -- confirmed by direct fetch of both files via `gh api repos/camel-ai/camel/contents/...`.
importance: 17,400+ GitHub stars; NeurIPS 2023 paper (Li, Hammoud, Itani, Khizbullin, Ghanem, KAUST) that established the 'role-playing'/multi-agent-society paradigm widely cited as a foundational reference in the multi-agent-LLM literature; CAMEL also underlies the related social-simulation project OASIS (see below).

name: AI Diplomacy (frontier LLMs playing the negotiation board game Diplomacy)
venue: media/LessWrong-adjacent attention (widely shared AI-Twitter project, not a peer-reviewed venue)
uses: OpenRouter is used to give distinct LLMs (Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek, Llama, Qwen) control of the seven Diplomacy powers so they can negotiate, ally, betray, and issue orders against each other -- producing the actual game transcripts/results that were widely shared as evidence of emergent LLM negotiation/deception behavior.
openrouter_evidence: ai_diplomacy/clients.py defines `class OpenRouterClient(BaseModelClient)` with `self.client = AsyncOpenAI(base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1", api_key=self.api_key)` and a `ClientType.OPENROUTER` enum entry; README.md example default model config assigns `"ITALY": "openrouter-meta-llama/llama-4-maverick", "TURKEY": "openrouter-google/gemini-2.5-flash-preview-05-20"` -- confirmed via direct fetch of both files.
importance: 684 GitHub stars, actively maintained; this negotiation-game project (frontier models play Diplomacy) drew substantial AI-community/media attention as one of the most-cited public demonstrations of multi-model negotiation, alliance-building, and betrayal behavior in LLMs.

name: Scaling Laws For Scalable Oversight (oversight games: Debate, Mafia, Backdoor Code, Wargames)
uses: OpenRouter is the sole LLM access layer for two of the four oversight games studied: the Debate game (weak judge overseeing a debate between two stronger models) and the Mafia game (multi-agent social-deduction game used to derive oversight Elo scaling laws), producing the actual results/figures in the paper.
openrouter_evidence: debate/debate_new.py: `openrouter_api_key = os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"); client = OpenAI(base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1", api_key=openrouter_api_key)`; mafia/Mafia.py has the identical pattern plus a comment '# Helper function to call the LLM via OpenRouter Chat API' -- both confirmed via direct fetch.
importance: NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight paper from Max Tegmark's lab at MIT (Engels, Baek, Kantamneni, Tegmark) on scalable oversight -- directly in the debate-for-truth / social-deduction-game lane, deriving oversight scaling laws for Debate and Mafia among other games; repo is the official author-released code.

name: OASIS (Open Agent Social Interaction Simulations with One Million Agents)
uses: OASIS is a large-scale generative-agent social-media simulator (up to one million agents on Twitter/Reddit-like platforms) built directly on top of CAMEL's model layer, used to study opinion dynamics, group polarization, and information spread among LLM agents.
openrouter_evidence: docs/key_modules/models.mdx states 'OASIS supports all models listed in camel here: https://docs.camel-ai.org/key_modules/models' and shows `ModelFactory.create(model_platform=ModelPlatformType...)` as the model-instantiation pattern shared with CAMEL, whose enums.py/examples confirm native OpenRouter support (ModelPlatformType.OPENROUTER). No OASIS-specific example script using OpenRouter was found in the repo (only OpenAI/Qwen/vLLM examples), so this is inherited/documented support rather than a demonstrated run.
importance: 4,938 GitHub stars; prominent large-scale generative social-simulation project from the CAMEL-AI team (Yuan et al.), directly in the generative-agents-style social-simulation lane; importance is high but OpenRouter usage specifically for OASIS's own published results is not directly evidenced, only inherited framework capability.

name: OpenAI Hallucinations/SimpleQA experiments (Nature 2026 paper code)
uses: Data generation + labeling for the paper's core experiments: queries 4 frontier models (Gemini 3 Pro, GPT-5.2, Grok-4, Claude Opus 4.5) on the full SimpleQA test set under different abstention/penalty regimes via OpenRouter, then grades/labels every response with GPT-4.1 as an LLM-judge using the SimpleQA grader template. Judged outputs directly produce the paper's hallucination/abstention/accuracy figures.
importance: Official OpenAI-authored repo (org: openai) for a paper published in Nature (Kalai, Nachum, Vempala, Zhang, 2026), 'Evaluating large language models for accuracy incentivizes hallucinations' — a flagship OpenAI research result on why accuracy-only grading incentivizes hallucination. High-authority source (OpenAI research team) and high-visibility venue.
openrouter_evidence: lm.py: module docstring 'Lightweight OpenRouter-backed language model wrapper with on-disk caching'; OPENROUTER_MODELS = {'gemini-3-pro-preview': {'id': 'google/gemini-3-pro-preview',...}, 'grok-4': {'id':'x-ai/grok-4',...}, 'gpt-5':..., 'opus-4.5':{'id':'anthropic/claude-opus-4.5',...}}; self.api_key = os.environ.get('OPENROUTER_API_KEY'). README.md: 'Models queried (through OpenRouter API): google/gemini-3-pro-preview, openai/gpt-5.2, x-ai/grok-4, anthropic/claude-opus-4.5' and 'Grading: uses openai/gpt-4.1 with the SimpleQA grader template', with a documented per-model OpenRouter API cost table (~$2,778 total spend).

name: Nous Research — Autoreason (self-refinement with AI-judge panels)
uses: RLAIF-style synthetic-data pipeline: generates competing synthetic revisions (critic/author-B/synthesizer agents) each iteration and uses multi-model judge panels (Borda-count voting) to select/label the winning version. Key paper results — the 5-tier model-capability scaling curve and the cross-model-judge robustness check (testing whether findings are an artifact of same-model judge bias) — route Llama-3.1-8B, Gemini 2.0/2.5 Flash, and GPT-4o through OpenRouter via litellm's `openrouter/` prefix.
importance: Authored by Nous Research, a leading open-weight-model lab (Hermes model series, DisTrO, Forge) with a large following in the open-source/alignment community. Repo has 591 stars. Directly on-topic for RLAIF: synthetic revision generation + AI-feedback judge selection is exactly the methodology this lane targets.
openrouter_evidence: experiments/v2/run_capability_curve.py: MODELS = {'gemini_flash': 'openrouter/google/gemini-2.0-flash-001', 'llama8b': 'openrouter/meta-llama/llama-3.1-8b-instruct'}, called via litellm.acompletion(model=model_id,...). experiments/v2/run_cross_model_judges.py: JUDGE_MODELS = {'gpt4o': 'openrouter/openai/gpt-4o', 'gemini_flash': 'openrouter/google/gemini-2.5-flash-preview'}, NUM_JUDGES=7 per model, used to run 'a 7-judge panel using a specific model' feeding results_cross_model_judges/.

name: EQ-Bench Creative Writing Bench + Judgemark-v2 (LLM-judge labeling harness)
venue: community benchmark/leaderboard (eqbench.com); widely referenced in LLM-judge-quality discussions
uses: AI-feedback labeling pipeline: generates model completions for 32 creative-writing prompts, then has a judge model (OpenRouter-backed, recommended Claude Sonnet) rubric-score and pairwise-rank them (Glicko-2 Elo) to produce the public EQ-Bench Creative Writing leaderboard. The companion repo Judgemark-v2 reuses the same OpenRouter-backed harness but flips the object of study: it meta-evaluates how good various models are AS judges (numeric rubric scoring stability/separability across ~20 judge models), i.e. it generates/labels judge-quality data.
importance: Created by Samuel Paech (EQ-Bench). EQ-Bench / Creative-Writing-Bench is one of the most widely cited 'vibes'/creative-writing and LLM-judge-quality benchmarks in the open LLM community — routinely referenced in model release threads, r/LocalLLaMA, and discussions of LLM-judge bias. Combined ~580+ stars across the EQ-bench org's core repos (EQ-Bench 442, creative-writing-bench 113, Judgemark-v2 28).
openrouter_evidence: creative-writing-bench/.env.example: TEST_API_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions and JUDGE_API_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions (separate OpenRouter endpoints for the model-under-test and the judge model). Judgemark-v2/README.md setup instructions: 'export OPENAI_API_URL="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions"' as the judge-model API endpoint, run via `python judgemark_v2.py --judge-models "openai/gpt-4o-mini" ...`.

name: Bespoke Curator (synthetic-data curation library; underlies OpenThoughts/Bespoke-Stratos/s1K reasoning-distillation datasets)
venue: widely used open-source tool (Bespoke Labs); underlying datasets referenced across many 2025-2026 reasoning-distillation papers
uses: General synthetic-data-curation/distillation library for LLM post-training. Ships a maintained, production-quality example pulling DeepSeek-R1 reasoning traces through OpenRouter (with pinned provider order, require_parameters, and reasoning-token capture) to build teacher-distillation datasets. Curator itself is the tool used to build the influential OpenThoughts-114k, Bespoke-Stratos-17k, and s1K-1.1 reasoning-distillation datasets, though I could not confirm those specific public dataset releases used the OpenRouter backend rather than a direct provider API — flagging as medium confidence for that reason.
importance: 1,702 GitHub stars; built by Bespoke Labs. The datasets it produced (OpenThoughts-114k, Bespoke-Stratos-17k, s1K-1.1) are heavily cited/reused across the 2025 wave of open reasoning-model distillation work (DeepSeek-R1-style CoT distillation).
openrouter_evidence: examples/providers/openrouter_reasoning_online.py: class Reasoner(curator.LLM) with model_name='deepseek/deepseek-r1' (comment: 'https://openrouter.ai/deepseek/deepseek-r1'), backend_params={'base_url': 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1', 'api_key': os.environ.get('OPENROUTER_API_KEY'), ...}, generation_params include 'provider': {'order': ['Fireworks','Kluster'], 'allow_fallbacks': False, 'require_parameters': True} — parses response['choices'][0]['message']['reasoning'] into a 'deepseek_reasoning' training column.

name: OpenPipe ART (Agent Reinforcement Trainer) — teacher/student distillation example
uses: dev/sft/distillation.py implements the canonical teacher-via-router distillation pattern: pulls a completion from a large teacher model (z-ai/glm-5) via OpenRouter, wraps it as an SFT trajectory, and trains a smaller Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct student model on it. This is a maintained example/tutorial in the repo rather than the pipeline behind a specific published ART result — I could not confirm OpenRouter was used for OpenPipe's flagship published benchmark (ART·E email-search agent), so importance to actual published research results is weaker than the other candidates here.
importance: ART is a very widely used (10,485 stars) open-source library for RL/GRPO training of agents (Qwen, GPT-OSS, Llama, etc.), used across many teams doing agent-RL research. Flagged because the OpenRouter teacher-distillation path is a first-class, maintained feature of a high-star research-infra project, even though I could not tie it to one specific published dataset.
openrouter_evidence: dev/sft/distillation.py: 'if not os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"): raise ValueError(...)'; TEACHER_MODEL = 'z-ai/glm-5'; teacher_client = AsyncOpenAI(api_key=os.environ['OPENROUTER_API_KEY'], base_url='https://openrouter.ai/api/v1'); the teacher completion is wrapped into an art.Trajectory and used to art.TrainableModel.train_sft(...) the student.

name: ARC-AGI Benchmarking (ARC Prize Foundation)
venue: arXiv / media (ARC-AGI-2 paper; official ARC Prize leaderboard tooling)
uses: Runs baseline LLMs against ARC-AGI tasks to produce the official ARC-AGI leaderboard results; OpenRouter is one of several provider adapters (alongside OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Fireworks, Grok, xAI) selectable per-model in provider_config.yml/models.yml.
importance: Official evaluation harness for the ARC Prize / ARC-AGI benchmark created by François Chollet — arguably the most media-covered 'general reasoning' benchmark in AI (cited in OpenAI's o3 announcement, $1M+ prize purses, ARC-AGI-2 paper arXiv:2505.11831). 351 GitHub stars on the tooling repo itself; the benchmark's results are cited constantly in reasoning-model discourse.
openrouter_evidence: src/arc_agi_benchmarking/adapters/openrouter.py: `class OpenRouterAdapter(OpenAIBaseAdapter): def init_client(self): api_key = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"); ... return OpenAI(api_key=api_key, base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1")`. Also provider_config.yml sets `openrouter: rate: 400 / period: 60` as a dedicated rate-limit block, confirming it's an actively-used adapter, not dead code.

name: MathArena (ETH Zurich SRI Lab)
venue: ETH Zurich SRI Lab (Martin Vechev group); widely referenced on X/LessWrong for tracking new competition-math results
uses: Evaluates frontier LLMs on newly-released, contamination-free competition math problems (AIME, HMMT, etc.) essentially in real time as new competitions are released; OpenRouter is one of the api_type options used to query models and produce the published leaderboard.
importance: 271 stars; run by ETH Zurich's SRI lab, well-known for security/verification-adjacent ML research; MathArena's near-live tracking of model performance on brand-new competition math problems (avoiding training-data contamination) is frequently cited in AI-capability discourse as a trustworthy math benchmark.
openrouter_evidence: src/matharena/api_client.py: `elif self.api == "openrouter": self.api_key = os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"); self.base_url = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"` and the query dispatcher `elif self.api == "openrouter": return self._openai_query_with_tools(idx, query)`.

name: Aider (AI pair-programming CLI + polyglot code benchmark)
venue: Community/industry (47k+ GitHub stars); benchmark leaderboard widely cited in LLM-coding capability comparisons
uses: Aider ships a dedicated OpenRouter integration (model metadata/pricing cache compatible with litellm) used both by end users and by Aider's own 'polyglot' code-editing benchmark (benchmark/benchmark.py, swe_bench.py) that produces the widely-referenced Aider leaderboard comparing coding ability across frontier models.
importance: 47,473 GitHub stars — one of the most popular open-source AI coding tools. Its benchmark/leaderboard is a de facto community reference point for 'how good is model X at code,' regularly cited in blog posts and model release comparisons.
openrouter_evidence: aider/openrouter.py: 'This module keeps a local cached copy of the OpenRouter model list (downloaded from https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models) ... class OpenRouterModelManager: MODELS_URL = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models"'. Also present: aider/website/docs/llms/openrouter.md and tests/basic/test_openrouter.py, plus benchmark/ directory implementing the polyglot code benchmark that model configs (including openrouter/* model names) are run against.

name: openbench (Groq)
uses: Provider-agnostic eval harness (built on top of Inspect AI) covering 95+ benchmarks; ships a dedicated OpenRouter provider wrapper and includes reasoning/math/code evals: gpqa_diamond.py, multiple years of AIME (aime_2023_I through recent), math.py, mathvista.py, and a matharena/ subpackage mirroring ETH SRI's competition-math sets.
importance: Authored by Groq (major AI inference-hardware company); 784 stars; positions itself explicitly as 'provider-agnostic, open-source evaluation infrastructure for language models' with OpenRouter as a named first-class provider (`openrouter/model-name` prefix, OPENROUTER_API_KEY) in its README provider table.
openrouter_evidence: src/openbench/model/_providers/openrouter.py: `class OpenRouterAPI(OpenAICompatibleAPI): DEFAULT_BASE_URL = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"`, subclassing inspect_ai's OpenAICompatibleAPI, with docstring documenting OPENROUTER_API_KEY and OpenRouter provider-routing params (only/order/ignore/sort/max_price). README provider table row: '| OpenRouter | OPENROUTER_API_KEY | openrouter/model-name |'. Repo also contains src/openbench/evals/gpqa_diamond.py, evals/gpt_oss/aime.py, evals/matharena/aime_2023_I/aime_2023_I.py, evals/math.py, datasets/mathvista.py.
